channel.title: Encryption on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Encryption on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted PKI and Certificate Authority: cfssl, smallstep/step-ca, and EJBCA Comparison Guide
item.description: A self-hosted Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) and Certificate Authority (CA) gives you complete control over certificate issuance for your internal services, devices, and applications. While Let’s Encrypt and ACME-based tools handle public-facing certificates, a self-hosted CA is essential for internal TLS, mutual TLS (mTLS), client authentication, code signing, and IoT device identity. This guide compares three self-hosted CA solutions: Cloudflare’s cfssl, smallstep’s step-ca, and EJBCA.

item.title: Self-Hosted Email Encryption: PGP, S/MIME & End-to-End Encrypted Mail Solutions 2026
item.description: Email remains one of the most widely used communication protocols, yet most messages traverse the internet in plaintext. Self-hosted email encryption gives you full control over message confidentiality, ensuring only intended recipients can read your correspondence. This guide covers the leading self-hosted email encryption approaches — PGP, S/MIME, and integrated encrypted mail servers.

item.title: Mozilla SOPS vs git-crypt vs age: Encrypt Secrets in Git 2026
item.description: Storing configuration files in Git is a cornerstone of infrastructure-as-code and self-hosted server management. But configuration files often contain passwords, API keys, TLS certificates, and database credentials. Committing these in plaintext is a security risk that can lead to data breaches, especially in shared repositories.
